programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: Murdoch investigates the death of a man found in a burned-out truck and catches a glimpse of the impossible - a long-gone nemesis back from the dead

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Celebrity guests Carol Vorderman, Larry Lamb, Jo Brand and Russell Howard join team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack for the comedy panel show in which contestants try to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves. Rob Brydon presents

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Extended edition. Comedian, novelist and presenter David Baddiel, comedian, actress and writer Cariad Lloyd and stand-up comedian and actor Ross Noble join regular panellist Alan Davies and host Sandi Toksvig to contemplate questions and facts on the theme of nature and nurture. As usual, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window in this quiz, in which the questions are so difficult to crack that points are instead awarded for the answers deemed most interesting

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: In this special festive edition of the award-winning show, Tom Allen plays host in front of a packed audience and welcomes Thanyia Moore and Lloyd Griffith to the stage

programme.title: Mel Giedroyc: Unforgivable
programme.desc: Mel and Lou are joined by Mel Jamie Laing, Fern Brady and Jordan Stephens as Jamie and Jordan disclose their run-ins with the police

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Border Force: America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: A driver is stopped attempting to smuggle 15kg of cocaine across the border, and a truck driver's modified cab attracts attention at the World Trade Bridge

programme.title: Border Force: America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: A Cuban woman crosses the bridge in the hope of being granted political asylum in America, while officers in Laredo stop three students carrying something unusual in their car

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef embarks on a series of extended culinary breaks, visiting markets, restaurants, wineries, cafes and bars, looking for food excellence and also some innovative recipes that will encourage viewers to cook at home. He begins his first adventure in autumnal Bordeaux, where he helps judge a local wine contest and hires a 2CV for an excursion to the coast - abundant with mussels and oysters. At home, he cooks a memorable dish of steak frites with Bordelaise sauce

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef embarks on another round of extended culinary breaks, visiting markets, restaurants, wineries, cafes and bars, beginning with Portuguese capital Lisbon. The culinary effects of Portugal's historic explorations are still seen in its dishes with hints of cinnamon and freshly chopped coriander from the east and tomatoes and chillies from the Americas. Rick is inspired to cook salt cod fritters and an almond tart, before heading for the St Anthony's Day Parade and Sardine Festival

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Richard Hammond celebrates the Porsche 911 by taking the GT3 to its limits, James May tries out the BMW 5 series and Jeremy Clarkson tests Ford's supercar the GT40. Plus, a tribute to the endurance of the Toyota pickup, and a chance to see how the show's 20-year-old Jaguar finally met its end. Martin Kemp is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The team visit the Isle of Man to drive the BMW Z4, Porsche Boxster and Honda S2000 on the famous TT circuit and Jeremy Clarkson puts the Mazda RX8 through its paces. Stephen Fry is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car and a Volvo estate is tested to its limits in an attempt to see how many caravans it can jump

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Cameras follow old friends Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer as they embark on an angling trip round the UK. The first stop is Norfolk, and while fishing for tench, Bob tries to impress Paul with his childhood fishing rod - and fails. They share nostalgia for their younger years and reveal how they recently came face to face with their own mortality due to heart disease

programme.title: Turkey with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: In the second half of the journey, Simon travels along the Black Sea Coast to the capital of Ankara and the Northern Highlands, witnessing first hand the aftermath of fighting in Kurdish areas and meeting a conservationist attempting to preserve the region's brown bears. He then returns to Istanbul, where he takes part in a popular TV series set in the palace of the Ottoman Sultans

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers reflect on the 1960s, a decade of music, fashion and food trends that are still around today. They prepare steak Diane and profiteroles, meet a restaurateur who made eating out trendy and look back on a time when the motorway cafe was the height of sophisticated dining for some

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef embarks on a series of extended culinary breaks, visiting markets, restaurants, wineries, cafes and bars, looking for food excellence and also some innovative recipes that will encourage viewers to cook at home. He begins his first adventure in autumnal Bordeaux, where he helps judge a local wine contest and hires a 2CV for an excursion to the coast - abundant with mussels and oysters. programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing

programme.title: Border Force: America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: Vigilant Customs and Border Protection officers uncover 5,000 rounds of ammunition being smuggled across Hidalgo Bridge into Mexico

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef heads north towards the Arctic Circle to explore the culinary delights of Reykjavik, coastal capital of Iceland. His aim is to enjoy the perfect winter break, enriched with a smorgasbord featuring the best ingredients the country has to offer - fjord-reared roast lamb, rye bread baked in volcanic sand and langoustine soup. Rick also resumes his search for the perfect cod

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef visits the home of New Nordic cuisine - Copenhagen - where he goes foraging for rosehip and learns about the art of pickling buds, bark and flowers. He's cheered to find plenty of traditional favourites, such as the city's famous Smorrebrod open sandwiches on rye bread, delicious poached turbot with cabbage and lemon butter sauce and - of course - the ubiquitous Danish pastry. Back at home, Rick cooks fried pork belly with lovage potatoes and parsley sauce, which has been voted Denmark's favourite dish

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: James May goes for a spin in the Subaru Legacy, Richard Hammond tests Peugeot and Renault's coupe convertibles and Jeremy Clarkson looks at the changing face of Bentley with the Continental GT. Comic actor Rob Brydon is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car and the team attempt to find out the safest way to escape from a submerged vehicle

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson puts the Lamborghini Gallardo through its paces, Richard Hammond tests enduring classic the Miura, and James May gets behind the wheel of 1980s favourite the Countach. Plus, a series of tuned Mini Coopers are taken for a spin, and the team stages a unique car long jump competition. American comedian Rich Hall is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul and Bob fish for barbel in Hay-on-Wye, and sample the great outdoors in spectacular fashion when Mortimer decides to strip in celebration of their catch. Passing a graveyard, they muse about the future and chat to a local vicar about death and their own funerals. To lighten the mood, back in their wooden fishing cabin, Bob promises Paul a very special treat if they are successful with their angling efforts

programme.title: The Lakes with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The adventurer explores the impact of humans on the Lake District, discovering that the high number of visitors to Scafell Pike is damaging England's highest peak and stretching the resources of mountain rescue volunteers. He goes wild swimming in the crystal clear lake of Buttermere, before taking a look at the less-fortunate Derwent Water, one of the many lakes now clogged with invasive New Zealand pygmyweed

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers focus on British sheep, which have provided the nation with milk, meat and wool for thousands of years. They rustle up a hogget and barley stew, lamb chops with kidneys, and North African dish mechoui lamb, and explore the history of the hardy breed found on North Ronaldsay in the Orkney Islands

programme.title: Border Force: America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: A Cuban woman crosses the bridge in the hope of being granted political asylum in America, while officers in Laredo stop three students carrying something unusual in their car

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef embarks on a series of extended culinary breaks, visiting markets, restaurants, wineries, cafes and bars, looking for food excellence and also some innovative recipes that will encourage viewers to cook at home. He begins his first adventure in autumnal Bordeaux, where he helps judge a local wine contest and hires a 2CV for an excursion to the coast - abundant with mussels and oysters. At home, he cooks a memorable dish of steak frites with Bordelaise sauce

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef embarks on another round of extended culinary breaks, visiting markets, restaurants, wineries, cafes and bars, beginning with Portuguese capital Lisbon. The culinary effects of Portugal's historic explorations are still seen in its dishes with hints of cinnamon and freshly chopped coriander from the east and tomatoes and chillies from the Americas. Rick is inspired to cook salt cod fritters and an almond tart, before heading for the St Anthony's Day Parade and Sardine Festival

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Richard Hammond celebrates the Porsche 911 by taking the GT3 to its limits, James May tries out the BMW 5 series and Jeremy Clarkson tests Ford's supercar the GT40. Plus, a tribute to the endurance of the Toyota pickup, and a chance to see how the show's 20-year-old Jaguar finally met its end. Martin Kemp is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The team visit the Isle of Man to drive the BMW Z4, Porsche Boxster and Honda S2000 on the famous TT circuit and Jeremy Clarkson puts the Mazda RX8 through its paces. Stephen Fry is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car and a Volvo estate is tested to its limits in an attempt to see how many caravans it can jump

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Cameras follow old friends Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer as they embark on an angling trip round the UK. The first stop is Norfolk, and while fishing for tench, Bob tries to impress Paul with his childhood fishing rod - and fails. They share nostalgia for their younger years and reveal how they recently came face to face with their own mortality due to heart disease

programme.title: Turkey with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: In the second half of the journey, Simon travels along the Black Sea Coast to the capital of Ankara and the Northern Highlands, witnessing first hand the aftermath of fighting in Kurdish areas and meeting a conservationist attempting to preserve the region's brown bears. He then returns to Istanbul, where he takes part in a popular TV series set in the palace of the Ottoman Sultans

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers reflect on the 1960s, a decade of music, fashion and food trends that are still around today. They prepare steak Diane and profiteroles, meet a restaurateur who made eating out trendy and look back on a time when the motorway cafe was the height of sophisticated dining for some

As usual, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window in this quiz, in which the questions are so difficult points are awarded for the answers its questionmaster finds most interesting

programme.title: Dave Gorman: Modern Life Is Goodish
programme.desc: The comedian performs a series of witty stand-up shows, beginning with jokes about the messages people receive every day

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: When popular local radio host Ryan Osbourne is killed in an explosion at his old high school, the shockwaves rattle the community as a fundraising initiative ends in tragedy. The hidden rivalries and secrets that emerge create a long list of suspects for Shepherd. Drama, starring Neill Rea

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: The stabbing of a woman in the street leads Klein to a Rwandan war criminal

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Heston agrees to advance Daniel £1,000, then calls a meeting with the doctors to announce that he has hired Lily as the new partner and is reducing appointment times to five minutes, much to the dismay of everybody else. Meanwhile, George and Ronnie continue to argue about their holiday

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Dr Lily Hassan (Seeta Indrani) begins work at the Mill, and Heston decides to give her Daniel's consulting room. Her first case is a woman concerned about a recurring nightmare that her fiance is dead, and the situation is compounded when the man in question visits the surgery with chest pains

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: During a short-staffed bank holiday, Connie tries to persuade the board to ask for Zubin's resignation. Diane insists on working, despite feeling sick, but later collapses and is rushed into theatre - where she loses the baby. Ric tries to make peace with Jess, but a surprise revelation causes him to change his mind, and Mickie comes to a decision about her future. Former EastEnders actor Gary Beadle (Paul Trueman) guest stars as a man who saves his dead brother's wife from a burning building

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Ruth helps a woman come to terms with her husband's terminal illness. Meanwhile, Dylan and Sam put aside their differences to diagnose a mystery condition and enable a teenage mother to keep her baby. Guest starring Susan Hampshire and Michael Jayston

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: The stabbing of a woman in the street leads Klein to a Rwandan war criminal

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: As Peggy resumes ownership of the Vic, Phil continues to sink into oblivion. The fists and fireworks fly at Janine's hen do as Bianca and Ronnie go head to head

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: It is Janine and Ryan's wedding day, but will the day go without a hitch? Peggy ignores a captive Phil's pleas as she prepares for the Vic party

programme.title: Harry Wild
programme.desc: The Vibrant Villages competition is fierce, with dirty tricks and sabotage galore, but Harry's friend Evelyn Summers makes a far deadlier discovery

programme.title: The Doctor Blake Mysteries
programme.desc: Patrick Tyneman's son Edward, recently released from jail, is arrested for the murder of a freemason. Everyone suspects Edward, but Blake must put his former rivalry with the Tynemans aside as he investigates, with the help of his former police colleague Doug Ashby

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: The police suspect Lovejoy of stealing from one of Jane's old friends, forcing him to enlist the help of refuse collector extraordinaire Brian Nun to clear himself. Unfortunately, matters are complicated by Eric's 'bin diving' habit. Light-hearted drama, starring Ian McShane and Phyllis Logan

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Tom becomes depressed after suffering a mild heart attack, so the management enlists Diana's help to try to cheer him up. Graham Crowden and Stephanie Cole star

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Ivy receives an anonymous love letter and spends all her time trying to work out who sent it, little realising Compo, Clegg and Foggy already know. However, she soon rules out everyone except Smiler - and promptly resolves to put him straight once and for all

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Howard concocts a plan to allay Pearl's well-founded suspicions - he just needs to find someone as small as himself. Comedy, starring Bill Owen, Peter Sallis and Brian Wilde

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Smiler decides to provide a service to society by becoming a lollipop man, but unfortunately he does not start on good terms with the locals - a problem compounded by Foggy's decision to give him a crash course in aggression. Rural comedy, starring Stephen Lewis and Brian Wilde

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: A young, handsome and charismatic priest joins the parish, breathing new life into the church and boosting numbers - until a sudden death changes everything

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The death of a teenage tennis star occupies the team when it is suggested her suicide jump two years earlier was anything but. They soon find several people with motives for her murder, including her coach Nick and agent Anthony, but it seems the one with the most to benefit from the girl's demise was her great rival Fawn Brammall - a shy and subdued player who proves difficult to question. Meanwhile, Gerry wonders if he did enough to encourage his own daughter's sporting ambitions and Brian hatches a plan to turn his dog into a movie star. Detective drama, guest starring Tamzin Outhwaite and Alexei Sayle

programme.title: Dalziel & Pascoe
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Tragedy strikes a leading football club when the team bus is hit by a train and the post-mortem examination reveals it was not an accident. Among the victims is manager Martin Bendelow, whose family has more than its fair share of skeletons in the cupboard, and more secrets are revealed when another murder takes place. Stephen Beckett guest stars, with Warren Clarke and Colin Buchanan

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: The police suspect Lovejoy of stealing from one of Jane's old friends, forcing him to enlist the help of refuse collector extraordinaire Brian Nun to clear himself. Unfortunately, matters are complicated by Eric's 'bin diving' habit. Light-hearted drama, starring Ian McShane and Phyllis Logan

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: Brokenwood is an unlikely beacon for extraterrestrial activity - until a local chef is found dead in a crop circle. Was it an alien abduction gone wrong?

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Proctor decides to trap a burglar as part of his plan to avoid being transferred from Sun Hill. Gregory Donaldson stars

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Jimmi helps a traumatised boy tell the police he saw his father being shot. The doctor takes him to visit his injured father in hospital, and ends up saving the child from being kidnapped by a gunman. Meanwhile, Heston holds a Hallowe'en party to welcome Lily to the practice

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Ruth is embarrassed to be seen leaving Daniel's after spending the night with him and surprised by Michelle's frosty reaction when she tries to share the gossip. Heston reduces Vivien to tears with his new system for managing reception, and Archie tries to help a comedian get his priorities straight

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Ric's ex-wife arrives at the hospital as the new registrar, quickly making her mark on the wards - putting Connie in her place as she does so. Carlos treats a patient suffering extensive burns, and Zubin awaits the board meeting that will decide his fate - but even though he is determined to resign, Jess persuades him to fight his corner. However, both are surprised when help emerges from an unexpected quarter

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Ruth's unexpected condition gives her cause to rethink her mental-health medication, but although Jay and Jordan encourage her to stop taking the lithium, she is petrified of having a relapse. Meanwhile, Dixie risks her life to save a man from a fire, and a drunken cancer patient reunites with his sister after seven years. Starring Georgia Taylor and Jane Hazlegrove

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Proctor decides to trap a burglar as part of his plan to avoid being transferred from Sun Hill. Gregory Donaldson stars

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Peggy and Phil's emotional showdown has disastrous consequences for everyone in the Vic. With Stacey and Lily's lives in peril, will Ryan be able to get to them in time?

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: The Mitchells are left devastated by last night's shocking events - will Albert Square ever recover from the tragedy?

programme.title: Harry Wild
programme.desc: On Glenn and Petra's wedding day, an ex-soldier with a bomb demands that Harry and Fergus solve the murder of his daughter, on the anniversary of her death

programme.title: The Doctor Blake Mysteries
programme.desc: Blake and Lawson investigate when a mechanic is found dead underneath a car. Australian detective drama, starring Craig McLachlan

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: Two mysterious murders appear to be the work of an ancient Mayan curse attached to a statue acquired by Lovejoy. Drama, starring Ian McShane and Phyllis Logan

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: The managers of Bayview Retirement Village threaten to expel Diana, prompting Tom to come up with a blackmail plot. Comedy, starring Stephanie Cole and Graham Crowden

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: A man researching a guidebook on cross-country walks foolishly accepts Foggy's offer of help, hoping the self-proclaimed survival expert's military training ensures his expedition runs like clockwork. Comedy, starring Brian Wilde, Peter Sallis and Bill Owen

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Compo, Clegg and Foggy learn the mysterious prophet Bickerdyke has foretold the end of the world - just as the rural calm is shattered by a series of explosions. Doomsday is just around the corner, or it could just be Wesley having car trouble. Comedy, starring Peter Sallis, Bill Owen and Brian Wilde

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Compo bets Foggy that he will succeed in getting Nora Batty to kiss him before the day is ended. Roy Clarke's long-running comedy, starring Bill Owen and Brian Wilde

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Monsieur Alphonse and Rene decide to duel with pistols to determine who should win Edith's hand, while Hitler orders the Colonel's arrest

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Rene, Hans and the Colonel are taken to the chateau's dungeons for interrogation at the hands of Herr Flick and his fiendish organ music. Meanwhile, the British airmen prepare to make their escape by balloon. Wartime comedy, starring Gorden Kaye, Richard Gibson and Richard Marner

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Herr Flick is informed that the Communists have kidnapped his sausage and are demanding a ransom, and a plan is devised for René to pay it

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Sandra and the team unearth the 10-year-old case of a poet from Belfast whose burnt body was found in the scrapyard of a known gangster. Gerry becomes convinced the victim's links with the criminal fraternity led to his demise, but Brian has a different theory, believing the secret of his murder lies in his verse. Amanda Redman, Dennis Waterman and Alun Armstrong star

programme.title: Dalziel & Pascoe
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Dalziel meets an attractive British singer during a European police conference, but wakes up to find her dead and himself accused of murder. Released on bail, he begins inquiries into the case which yield a complex story of diamond dealing and police corruption. Michael French guest stars

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: Two mysterious murders appear to be the work of an ancient Mayan curse attached to a statue acquired by Lovejoy. Drama, starring Ian McShane and Phyllis Logan

programme.title: Monsieur Spade
programme.desc: Spade interrogates the intruder and learns that there are people who still very much want him dead. Another body is discovered, and Teresa begins her own investigation

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Lennox's loyalties are tested when he sees his wife at the home of a suspect

programme.title: The Two Ronnies
programme.desc: Vintage comedy with Messrs Barker and Corbett, including the sketches News Items, Fatheads Revisited, Hell Fire, Tramps and One Man Band. Elaine Paige provides the music

programme.title: Wild China
programme.desc: Built to shelter ancient China from hostile barbarians to the north, the Great Wall also serves as a cultural curtain, beyond which can be found a variety of traditions. This programme goes outside the Wall to meet tribes of reindeer herders on the Siberian border, and explores deserts to the north where ruined cities were once bustling staging posts for camel trains on the legendary Silk Road

programme.title: Wild China
programme.desc: The series concludes with a visit to the country's eastern seaboard to investigate how the Chinese government is managing environmental concerns while meeting the ever-expanding needs of the population. Among the region's diverse locales lie futuristic cities, seaweed-thatched villages and rugged wetlands that are home to some of the world's rarest animal species

programme.title: Wild Scandinavia
programme.desc: Rebecca Ferguson narrates a documentary exploring the wildlife, landscape and culture of the northern European region, beginning with a coastal journey from the sheltered Baltic Sea to the wild Atlantic Ocean, travelling north to freezing Arctic waters. Seal pups are born on granite islands left by the Ice Age, while flocks of migrant birds visit the largest tidal flats on the planet, and Europe's largest eagles patrol Norway's sea cliffs

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Veteran actors Timothy West and Prunella Scales head to Llangollen, Denbighshire, where they spent their honeymoon. Reminiscing over how their relationship has survived and thrived over the past 50 years, the pair take in the picturesque sights of the Vale of Llangollen and the Montgomery Canal, before travelling on a horse-drawn barge up the Horseshoe Falls

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Timothy West and Prunella Scales visit the Burgundy region of France, where they travel along the Canal du Nivernais, which passes through towns and villages steeped in history. The duo also visit the market town of Chablis to help with the annual grape harvest and the medieval city of Auxerre before reflecting on what the journey has meant to them as a married couple

programme.title: Sue Perkins: Along the US-Mexico Border
programme.desc: Part one of two. The comedian travels along one of the world's most contentious borders, visiting places in America and Mexico to find out what life is like on both sides. Sue starts her journey at the western end of the border in the Mexican city of Tijuana, where she helps Honduran volunteers building a hostel for fellow refugees fleeing violence in their country

programme.title: Sue Perkins: Along the US-Mexico Border
programme.desc: Part two of two. Sue visits the city of Juarez, which adjoins El Paso on the other side of the border, the two effectively forming a single large city spreading across two nations. A 57-mile section of border wall runs through the two cities, but does not stop thousands of people coming and going across every day. Sue confronts her fear of horses to try out a Mexican form of dressage and meets a woman living in the US who has been separated from her husband, who is not legally allowed to enter the US

programme.desc: On a wartime bomber base in Lincolnshire, veteran engineer Gerry Cooper rebuilds two pre-war biplanes with glorious histories; the US Navy N3N and the Morane Saulnier 315

programme.title: Trawlermen
programme.desc: A vital chain gives way on the Ocean Venture's nets and Barry Lauder has to think quickly to save the day. Meanwhile on the Amity, skipper Jimmy Buchan ignores the concerns of his first mate and enters the jagged contours of the Ooze Hole to fish for prawns

programme.title: Trawlermen
programme.desc: The skipper of one of the smallest boats in the Peterhead fleet must decide whether to head back to harbour or brave the treacherous sea following a severe weather warning. programme.desc: Singer Elaine Paige guest stars with Messrs Barker and Corbett, performing Unchained Melody with accompaniment by the Zac Herbert Steel Band

programme.title: Sue Perkins: Along the US-Mexico Border
programme.desc: Part two of two. Sue visits the city of Juarez, which adjoins El Paso on the other side of the border, the two effectively forming a single large city spreading across two nations. A 57-mile section of border wall runs through the two cities, but does not stop thousands of people coming and going across every day. Sue confronts her fear of horses to try out a Mexican form of dressage and meets a woman living in the US who has been separated from her husband, who is not legally allowed to enter the US

programme.desc: The actor examines British innovations in the history of engineering. programme.title: David Jason's Great British Inventions
programme.desc: The actor examines British innovations in the history of flight, from the first glider to the modern jet suit, and discovers how Britain contributed to the moon landing

programme.title: David Jason's Great British Inventions
programme.desc: The actor operates one of the world's biggest hydraulic cranes, gets behind the wheel of a tank, and discovers the story behind the invention of the hovercraft

programme.title: David Jason's Great British Inventions
programme.desc: The actor examines British innovations in the home from the lightbulb to the lawnmower, and discovers the role Queen Victoria played in creating the telephone

programme.desc: Del seizes a rare opportunity to make a quick profit and bring Trotters Independent Traders into the hi-tech world of satellite communications. Meanwhile, Rodney plunges to the depths of despair as his marriage continues to deteriorate. Comedy, starring David Jason and Nicholas Lyndhurst

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: An audition gives Raquel the chance of a lifetime - and perhaps more importantly, an excuse to get out of Nelson Mandela House. Del-Boy is as excited as she is about the prospect, but only because he sees himself as the man behind her rapid rise to stardom. Comedy, starring David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Tessa Peake-Jones

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Ted falls in love with a piano-playing guest at the holiday camp, but romance proves difficult with her overbearing mother in the way. Classic comedy, starring Paul Shane, David Griffin and Ruth Madoc

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Alison and Mike receive some exciting news about their guesthouse business, but the fallout soon sees tensions running high between the pair. Pat and Thomas learn about the challenges of veganism, Kitty attempts to adopt a new way of communicating that sparks confusion between her and Alison. Lady Button is delighted by a new arrival that allows her to tap into a past passion. Robin however is less charmed by the new addition. Julian is confronted by a hard truth, but is determined to prove everyone wrong. Alison and Mike reach boiling point as Robin comes face to face with his biggest fear

programme.title: Ever Decreasing Circles
programme.desc: Martin's sense of authority begins to wane when the arrival of new neighbour Paul threatens the equilibrium in his community. programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles meets Kettering mum of four Lindsey, who married her ex-Royal Engineer husband Shaun in December 2019, after which they began to extend their Kettering home. But a few months later Shaun was struck down with cancer, and died in November 2021, leaving Lindsey and her children with an unfinished home. But help is on hand as the DIY SOS team, along with hundreds of local trades and several of Shaun's old army buddies, step in to finish the job

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team travel to Southmead in Bristol, where a charity-owned adventure playground known as the Ranch has fallen into disrepair and is now rendered unfit for purpose. However, that is about to change as local volunteers and tradespeople help to reinvigorate the site, creating a recording studio and forest school to sit alongside the playground and keep the kids happy for years to come

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team venture to Northumberland to finish a build they started just before the pandemic, helping a couple whose 18-year-old daughter has complex medical and mental health issues. Nick and designer Gabrielle Blackman put out the call to local trades and volunteers, who turn up en masse and completely redesign the house, add an extension and create a home that works for everyone

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip

programme.title: The Supervet

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team travel to Fegg Hayes in Stoke-on-Trent, where Sue's family have lived for six generations. To tackle poverty, isolation and mental health problems, they have taken over a patch of wasteland in the heart of the community, with the aim of creating a community garden and centre with room to grow vegetables and space where young and old can share knowledge and experiences. The DIY SOS team are determined to help get the job done, accompanied by hundreds of local tradespeople - only to run into the worst storms to hit Britain in recent years

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Strictly Come Dancing stars Anton Du Beke, Katya Jones and Graziano Di Prima join Nick Knowles and the team in Wallsend, Newcastle. Together, they set to work transforming the True Colours performing arts school, an inclusive dance school that teaches dance and theatre skills to 500 children and young adults weekly, many of whom have disabilities and learning needs. The school has grown substantially over time and they've completely outgrown their existing space, so the team get to work renovating a run-down old boys' club that would give the dance school the extra space it so desperately needs to continue to transform more young lives

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team travel to Essex, where local mental health charity Butterfly Effect Well-being is in desperate need of a new meeting place. They have an army of local volunteer trades - and the cast of EastEnders, who have made the trip from Walford to help transform a derelict building into a warm and welcoming space for the local community

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip

programme.desc: Countdown's Rachel Riley and her husband and former Strictly partner Pasha Kovalev compete, teaming up with Paul Martin and David Harper to help scour the shops of Essex. Rachel gets to grips with a longbow and discovers why English archers were so formidable in war during the Middle Ages, while Pasha's weapon of choice makes a bigger bang as he finds out how the town of Colchester was besieged during the English Civil War

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Beloved fluff monster Kylo, a 10-month-old rough collie, and golden retriever pup Sasha make return visits to the practice. Kylo's serious leg deformities are causing significant pain, while Sasha's deformed front left foot has left her without properly developed toes - and her struggles are increasing the more she grows. Professor Noel Fitzpatrick and his team need to find innovative solutions to resolve the problems both hounds present

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A 10-year-old Dobermann is treated for an aggressive bone tumour in its leg. One option is a full limb amputation, but another could potentially save Rolo's leg by cutting out the affected part of the bone and replacing it with a unique implant of Noel's own invention - a scapula-humeral endoprosthesis. Five-year-old labrador Jeff is suffering from developmental elbow disease, which cannot be managed by medication alone. The day of his planned surgery for a full elbow replacement arrives, though bad news places the prospect of such treatment in jeopardy

programme.title: Hashtag Homicide
programme.desc: In August 2021, travel influencer Gabby Petito vanished during a trip with her fiance. Her online posts helped armchair detectives trace her final movements

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Danny Dyer and daughter Dani join Natasha Raskin Sharp and Philip Serrell on an antiques hunt in Essex that features pirates, a raft race, sunken treasure and a Chinese banquet

programme.desc: Edina takes time out from being PR guru to the country's biggest stars to become involved with Patsy's fashion magazine. Cosmo and Vogue beware - two new lushes are in town. programme.title: Tipping Point

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles meets Kettering mum of four Lindsey, who married her ex-Royal Engineer husband Shaun in December 2019, after which they began to extend their Kettering home. But a few months later Shaun was struck down with cancer, and died in November 2021, leaving Lindsey and her children with an unfinished home. But help is on hand as the DIY SOS team, along with hundreds of local trades and several of Shaun's old army buddies, step in to finish the job

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team travel to Southmead in Bristol, where a charity-owned adventure playground known as the Ranch has fallen into disrepair and is now rendered unfit for purpose. However, that is about to change as local volunteers and tradespeople help to reinvigorate the site, creating a recording studio and forest school to sit alongside the playground and keep the kids happy for years to come

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team venture to Northumberland to finish a build they started just before the pandemic, helping a couple whose 18-year-old daughter has complex medical and mental health issues. Nick and designer Gabrielle Blackman put out the call to local trades and volunteers, who turn up en masse and completely redesign the house, add an extension and create a home that works for everyone

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: More famous faces embark on the cross-country treasure-hunting challenge, beginning with supermodel Jerry Hall and psychotherapist and TV presenter Philippa Perry taking to the road with antiques experts Tim Medhurst and Charles Hanson

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: The pioneering vet treats a 10-year-old beagle unable to stand on its back legs, and when a scan reveals the canine has two diseased discs between three of its neck vertebrae, he operates to fuse the bones. He also fits a bionic knee in an 11-year-old labrador with bone cancer in its leg, and treats a bulldog puppy that has been run over by a car

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A two-year-old dachshund is rushed to Fitzpatricks by a distraught friend of the owner after being run over by a driver and sustaining life-threatening injuries. A couple arrive with their nine-year-old schnauzer, which is booked in for complex spinal surgery after an earlier operation on its knee, and the owners of a shih tzu bring in their pet, which has been injured in an accident at home

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: The Yorkshire Ambulance Service's Hazardous Area Response Team tries to seal a major chemical spill and helps a teenager who fell down a chimney

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Football pundit Lee Dixon brings his beloved rescue dog to see Professor Noel Fitzpatrick, looking for help treating a limp and getting his pet back to full fitness. programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Countdown's Rachel Riley and her husband and former Strictly partner Pasha Kovalev compete, teaming up with Paul Martin and David Harper to help scour the shops of Essex. programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: The very first episode of the award-winning comedy kicks off with Edina and Patsy hitting the bottle as they struggle to cope with career and family pressures. Starring Jennifer Saunders and Joanna Lumley

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Edina is horrified when Bubble tells her she is about to be visited by Penny Caspar-Morse, an ultra-thin model she knew in the 1960s who used to torment her about her weight. She decides to lose some weight, but abandons her exercise programme and lets Patsy schedule her an appointment to have liposuction. Comedy, guest starring Alexandra Bastedo, with Jennifer Saunders and Joanna Lumley

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team travel to Fegg Hayes in Stoke-on-Trent, where Sue's family have lived for six generations. To tackle poverty, isolation and mental health problems, they have taken over a patch of wasteland in the heart of the community, with the aim of creating a community garden and centre with room to grow vegetables and space where young and old can share knowledge and experiences. The DIY SOS team are determined to help get the job done, accompanied by hundreds of local tradespeople - only to run into the worst storms to hit Britain in recent years

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Strictly Come Dancing stars Anton Du Beke, Katya Jones and Graziano Di Prima join Nick Knowles and the team in Wallsend, Newcastle. Together, they set to work transforming the True Colours performing arts school, an inclusive dance school that teaches dance and theatre skills to 500 children and young adults weekly, many of whom have disabilities and learning needs. The school has grown substantially over time and they've completely outgrown their existing space, so the team get to work renovating a run-down old boys' club that would give the dance school the extra space it so desperately needs to continue to transform more young lives

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team travel to Essex, where local mental health charity Butterfly Effect Well-being is in desperate need of a new meeting place. They have an army of local volunteer trades - and the cast of EastEnders, who have made the trip from Walford to help transform a derelict building into a warm and welcoming space for the local community

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Beloved fluff monster Kylo, a 10-month-old rough collie, and golden retriever pup Sasha make return visits to the practice. Kylo's serious leg deformities are causing significant pain, while Sasha's deformed front left foot has left her without properly developed toes - and her struggles are increasing the more she grows. Professor Noel Fitzpatrick and his team need to find innovative solutions to resolve the problems both hounds present

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A 10-year-old Dobermann is treated for an aggressive bone tumour in its leg. One option is a full limb amputation, but another could potentially save Rolo's leg by cutting out the affected part of the bone and replacing it with a unique implant of Noel's own invention - a scapula-humeral endoprosthesis. Five-year-old labrador Jeff is suffering from developmental elbow disease, which cannot be managed by medication alone. The day of his planned surgery for a full elbow replacement arrives, though bad news places the prospect of such treatment in jeopardy

programme.title: The Battle of Britain
programme.desc: Actor Ewan McGregor and his brother Colin, an RAF pilot, retell the story of the 1940 air battle that proved to be a turning point in the Second World War. The duo also meet some of those who participated in the conflict itself

programme.desc: The historian follows in the footsteps of 19th-century gold prospectors, attempting to find riches in a remote region on the edge of the Canadian arctic. programme.title: Tankies: Tank Heroes of WW2
programme.desc: Part one of two. BBC diplomatic editor and former army officer Mark Urban examines the exploits of six members of the 5th Royal Tank Regiment during the Second World War, beginning in northern France, where in 1940 rookie driver Gerry Solomon joined veterans including Jake Wardrop and Harry Finlayson. He follows in the soldiers' tracks across the deserts of North Africa, where he looks at the battles of Sidi Rezegh, Alam el Halfa and El Alamein, which changed the course of the conflict. With archive footage, including rarely seen colour film, as well as first-hand testimony from surviving veterans and letters and diaries

programme.title: Tankies: Tank Heroes of WW2
programme.desc: The two-part documentary telling the stories of six men who served in the Royal Tank Regiment concludes, with the men taking part in the D-Day landings, the battle for Normandy and the eventual victory in Germany

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow

programme.title: Tankies: Tank Heroes of WW2
programme.desc: Part one of two. BBC diplomatic editor and former army officer Mark Urban examines the exploits of six members of the 5th Royal Tank Regiment during the Second World War, beginning in northern France, where in 1940 rookie driver Gerry Solomon joined veterans including Jake Wardrop and Harry Finlayson. He follows in the soldiers' tracks across the deserts of North Africa, where he looks at the battles of Sidi Rezegh, Alam el Halfa and El Alamein, which changed the course of the conflict. With archive footage, including rarely seen colour film, as well as first-hand testimony from surviving veterans and letters and diaries

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: A look at the Airbus A380, which as the largest passenger plane ever built can carry more than 850 people non-stop nearly half-way around the world. With the largest wingspan of any commercial aircraft and four specially developed mighty turbo jet engines, the A380 weighs 560 tonnes and getting it off the ground would not have been possible without the work of past innovators such as George Cayley and Frank Whittle. Key developments in aerospace engineering - from Richard Whitcombe's energy-efficient wing-tip designs to Nasa engineers' cutting-edge control system, Fly by Wire - helped bring the A380 to fruition

programme.title: The Game
programme.desc: Former detective Huw settles into retirement alongside his wife Alice, but never far from his mind is the case he failed to solve - that of the Ripton Stalker. When new neighbour Patrick moves in and uses the phrase that the stalker used to taunt Huw with in letters, he's determined he's finally got his man. Thriller, starring Jason Watkins, Robson Green and Sunetra Sarker

programme.title: Code of Silence
programme.desc: Determined to keep her meeting with Liam secret, Alison doesn't tell the police about him taking her to A&E. The police eventually find out about Alison's meeting with Liam and fire her due to her reckless actions, but she decides to take control of her own destiny when Liam invites her to a gang-linked meeting in London.

programme.title: Code of Silence
programme.desc: Alison Brooks, a 29-year-old deaf woman, works in the canteen of Kent and Canterbury police station, where her lip-reading skills thrust her into a high-stakes police investigation. Recruited by DI James Marsh and DS Ashleigh Francis, Alison proves invaluable as she assists in surveilling a gang planning a major heist, while she also has to navigate her mother's financial struggles, a broken relationship, and the ableist attitudes she encounters at work. Crime drama, starring Rose Ayling-Ellis, Andrew Buchan and Charlotte Ritchie

programme.title: The Six O'Clock Show

programme.sub-title: The One with All the Resolutions

programme.desc: Emily Atack and Danny Dyer host as the strategic quiz reaches its conclusion. A lavish operatic final challenge grants the winner the power to eliminate a rival outright, while a long-standing moral compass finally slips, resulting in a bold decision that shocks the house. The show culminates in a tense head-to-head as one player outmanoeuvres their opponent to win

programme.title: Harrods: The Rise & Fall of a British Institution
programme.desc: The history of arguably the most famous shop in the world, which has been based on Brompton Road in London for more than 175 years, employs more than 6,000 people and still welcomes 15 million customers every year. This documentary tells the story of the people behind the department store, including Robin Harrod, the great-great-grandson of the store's founder, and culminates with the recent allegations against former chairman Mohamed Al-Fayed

programme.title: The Way
programme.desc: With the town under martial law, Dee and Geoff try to find Owen who, denied medication, is hallucinating in an isolation camp. Thea discovers that a new security unit at the police station is trawling footage of the protest, and she notices that Owen is being singled out. Husband Dan is increasingly concerned about getting his family out. With Luke Evans

Crime drama, starring Tom Riley

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner simplify traditional Christmas dishes that can be a bit tricky to get right, including John's foolproof recipe for French toast muffins with hollandaise sauce. programme.title: Code of Silence
programme.desc: Alison's informant work grows riskier as she secretly follows Braden and Liam to a building site, where she learns that the gang need an insider to access the vault. As the police eventually identify the gang's target as billionaire Amir Joshi, Alison discovers a fact about her and Julie's looming eviction that pushes her to become increasingly conflicted about her bond with Liam

programme.title: Dark Heart

programme.title: Ireland AM
programme.desc: Magazine programme blending lifestyle interviews and news content with human interest stories, consumer affairs, health, politics, gardening, education, finance, food and fashion

programme.title: Anthony
programme.desc: In July 2005, 18-year-old Anthony Walker was murdered by two white men in an unprovoked racist attack in a Liverpool park. Jimmy McGovern's drama imagines what life may have held for Anthony if the teenager had not been killed, with the reverse chronology seeing him realise his dreams and enjoy the life he had a right to before fate - and hate - took it all away. Toheeb Jimoh stars

programme.title: The Trial of Richard Satchwell
programme.desc: An investigation into the disappearance and murder of 44-year-old Cork woman Tina Satchwell. With exclusive interviews from her family, this documentary follows the case from Tina's sudden disappearance in March 2017, through the cold-case breakthrough six years later, to the dramatic trial that finally brought her husband, Richard Satchwell, to justice

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Armed robbers target post offices around Aidensfield and Mason finds himself working with old flame Rachel Dawson, whose career is under threat following an affair with a senior colleague. Carol is not impressed when Rachel is spotted in a clinch with Joe, but he claims it was in the line of duty and completely innocent. Peggy offers to clean for Edward Walton (played by former Likely Lads actor Rodney Bewes), raising Oscar's suspicion she is after his money, but it is Edward's wife, now in a nursing home, who turns out to have a shady past

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Wealthy industrialist Sir Reuben Astwell is murdered after making a deal to sell synthetic rubber to the Germans, leaving the dapper sleuth wondering whether the motive was commercial or political. Evidence found in his chemical factory could hold the key to the mystery - but Poirot is unsure if the victim's widow can be trusted. Starring David Suchet, with Ann Bell and Bill Wallis

programme.title: The Instant Gardener
programme.desc: Presenter Helen Skelton and expert garden designer Danny Clarke transform a tiny backyard into a beach garden inspired by the neighbouring Barry coastline

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner prepare a menu perfect for a special night in, with blinis and a variety of topping, a classic prawn cocktail, and a toffee vodka espresso martini. The main course is a one-pot chicken dish packed with French flavours, while dessert is a giant chocolate souffle

programme.title: Peter Andre's 60 Minute Makeover
programme.desc: Peter and his army of helpers will be surprising deserving people across the country with a stunning home mkeover - performed in a day and brought to you in 60 minutes. In each episode one deserving person will get the surprise home makeover of a lifetime. Nominated by their friends and family they are everyday heroes with moving stories that tug at the heart.

programme.title: Alex Polizzi: Chefs on Trial
programme.desc: The troubleshooter tackles Amelies, a harbour-side seafood restaurant in the picturesque Cornish town of Porthleven. Owner Sam Sheffield Dunstan gave up a high-flying career in London five years ago to fulfil a lifetime ambition of opening an eatery. However, her dream turned into a nightmare as she failed to attract the right head chef, so a desperate Sam turned to Alex to help find the best person for the job. In this edition, Alex puts the first candidates through their paces

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A man begins receiving nuisance calls after a mysterious stranger moves into the village. Meanwhile, Gina's love life hots up in the kitchen - with serious repercussions. Rural police drama, starring Nick Berry and Derek Fowlds

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: A look at the history of personal flight, culminating in Richard Browning and his team of engineers' development of the Gravity Industries Jet Suit

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Ruth Watson revisits Worlington Hall in the Suffolk countryside to see if owners Joy and Sharon have taken her advice on how to save their failing business. On her first trip, she pinpointed an unwelcoming reception, a messy booking system, a confusing food policy and lack of publicity as the main problems impeding its success

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Gabby attempts to seduce Ross, but he rejects her advances, and Kerry agrees to look after Archie while Jai heads to Canada

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alya warns Leanne about Idris, Daniel celebrates a promising sign, Ryan points Lisa towards Danielle, and one resident wrestles with their conscience after lying about their movements on the night of the murder

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: Frankie accidentally injures a man attempting to steal his motorbike, but is stricken with guilt when the thief dies after crashing the vehicle. A woman being treated for severe burns disappears home, where Drs Ormerod and Weatherill discover her family secret, while Alun becomes obsessed by an expensive stereo system. With Scott Taylor, Robert Daws and Amy Robbins

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: Oxford plays host to a veteran film star, in town to visit a local cinema. However, a mysterious death casts a sinister pall over the occasion, and offers the newly promoted DS Endeavour Morse a fresh case to pursue. Meanwhile, a family visit puts DCI Thursday's hospitality to the test. Inspector Morse prequel, starring Shaun Evans and Roger Allam

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Jo makes the rash assumption that a man has taken violent steps to deal with his truanting son, and Greengrass sets up an illegal stud with a horse-training pal. Meanwhile, PC Ventress starts behaving oddly. Rural police drama, starring Nick Berry and Bill Maynard

programme.title: Cold Feet
programme.desc: Heartbreak continues to dog Rachel and Adam as a false alarm prompts them to seek specialist advice, concerned cancer treatment may have left him infertile, while Jenny's life takes an upward turn as she succumbs to the potent charms of Internet millionaire Robert. Pete, however, finds himself out on his ear, his misery compounded by a tardy revelation about his new landlord, and David discovers a cause worth fighting for

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Daniel suffers an emotional meltdown upon his return to work, Carl's suspicion unnerves Tyrone, Idris suggests another night out to Leanne, and Summer's dire situation upsets Todd

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Laurel succumbs to her passion for Ross, while Kerry and Pollard present a united front and Sam is desperate for more dance lessons.

programme.title: Black Work
programme.desc: A suspect is taken into custody for Ryan's murder, but Jo is not convinced the police have the guilty party. She continues to investigate her husband's undercover work, discovering dark secrets relating to both his colleagues and the criminals they were pursuing, and finally coming to a disturbing revelation about the man she married

programme.title: Showtrial
programme.desc: The trial gets underway. As the prosecution determine to prove beyond doubt that Talitha and Dhillon murdered Hannah Ellis together, Cleo must try and mitigate the forensic and witness evidence quickly stacking up against her client. Meanwhile, a revelation damages Dhillon's case and makes Meera look at her son in a new light. Questions over the admissibility of evidence cause clashes between the legal teams, and as tensions rise, Cleo and Damian fall out over whether or not to call Talitha as a witness

programme.title: Don't Look Back in Anger
programme.desc: Archive news documentary looking back at the headline stories of the past 20 years, focusing this week on 2009

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Eileen Needham sues for divorce after a domestic dispute gets out of hand, and her hard-nosed solicitor Sylvia Swinton (played by Outnumbered actress Samantha Bond) does little to calm the situation, so Joe steps in to play the peacemaker. Stan Bickle arrives in Aidensfield, bringing his daughter Josie (guest star Jenna Boyd), whose flirtatious ways steal David's heart and bring out the worst in Peggy

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A mysterious delivery causes Poirot to recall the time his supper at a Buenos Aires restaurant was rudely interrupted by the murder of a fellow diner, who unwittingly drank from a glass laced with cyanide. Drama, starring David Suchet and Philip Jackson

programme.title: The Instant Gardener
programme.desc: Helen Skelton and garden designer Danny Clarke help an urban community farm in Southampton create a sheltered seating area, with a fire-pit for marshmallow toasting

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner present their foolproof chocolate fondant - a great way to use up any leftover Easter eggs. Then it's a quick, easy and delicious French toast omelette, a main course of herb-crusted leg of lamb and a side dish of roasted leeks topped with creamy burrata and a parmesan crumb

programme.title: Peter Andre's 60 Minute Makeover
programme.desc: Peter and his army of helpers will be surprising deserving people across the country with a stunning home mkeover - performed in a day and brought to you in 60 minutes. In each episode one deserving person will get the surprise home makeover of a lifetime. Nominated by their friends and family they are everyday heroes with moving stories that tug at the heart.

programme.title: Alex Polizzi: Chefs on Trial
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi puts three more head chef candidates through their paces as they compete for a vacancy at Amelies, a seafood restaurant in Porthleven, Cornwall. With one candidate already through to the final, the next three applicants must endure a gruelling interview process. Amiable Irishman Barry hopes to impress with a main course of hare and rainbow pasta, while 20-year-old Chris wonders whether his decision to put a duo of lamb on a seafood menu might prove to be his undoing. Plus, experienced chef Paul refuses to let his work speak for itself as he tries to convince owner Sam he is the right man for the job

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Jo makes the rash assumption that a man has taken violent steps to deal with his truanting son, and Greengrass sets up an illegal stud with a horse-training pal. Meanwhile, PC Ventress starts behaving oddly. Rural police drama, starring Nick Berry and Bill Maynard

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: An insight into a team of engineers in Perth's attempts to not only break the 763mph land speed record set back in 1997, but also go past the 1,000mph barrier

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Ruth Watson tries to reverse the fortunes of struggling hotels around Britain, starting in Hastings, East Sussex , where Peter Mann's 17-room Grand Hotel is rapidly losing business to cheaper rivals. Ruth encourages him to make the dining room more appealing by clearing the clutter and creating a quasi-museum, and orders a series of renovations before the relaunch party

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Laurel succumbs to her passion for Ross, while Kerry and Pollard present a united front and Sam is desperate for more dance lessons.

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Daniel suffers an emotional meltdown upon his return to work, Carl's suspicion unnerves Tyrone, Idris suggests another night out to Leanne, and Summer's dire situation upsets Todd

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: Adam is quick to question Dr Weatherill's competence when a beach outing ends tragically for a family. Dr Ormerod leaps in to defend her - but ends up revealing his colleague's condition in his haste. Meanwhile, Dr Goodwin struggles to help a pair of teenage parents gain access to their baby. Amy Robbins and Robert Daws star

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: Morse suspects a connection between a woman's disappearance and the unsolved murder of a teenager several years previously. However, the discovery of a body and some unusual elements to the crime scene suggest another possibility. Thursday investigates a lorry hijack which he believes could be the work of a local gangster, and Fancy takes on an informant who is subsequently murdered

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick investigates the theft of thousands of pounds' worth of wine from a warehouse. Meanwhile, trustworthy Greengrass becomes a gamekeeper and the truth behind Alf's strange behaviour is revealed. Rural police drama set in the rustic backwater of Aidensfield, starring Nick Berry and Bill Maynard

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: Wealthy betting shop tycoon Alun Wilmott is shot and killed during a home invasion, leaving his wife and daughter traumatised - with large amounts of cash missing from his personal safe, it is assumed he is the unlucky victim of a burglary. Vera suspects an inside job in the shape of Alun's head of private security, Ciaran Duggan, whose history of violent crime calls his reformed character into question. Can the team find the evidence to pin the crime on their prime suspect - or are they looking in the wrong place? Brenda Blethyn stars, with guest appearances from Karl Collins, Patrick Robinson and Katy Carmichael

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Daniel unmasks his online troll, but neglects Bertie in the fallout, Lisa makes a breakthrough, David is thrown by Jodie's claim, and Alya encourages Adam to take on Idris as a client

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Ross is chuffed to contemplate another night with Laurel, while Kim's suspicions about Sam's behaviour continue to grow. Chas and Charity hatch a plan to burgle the Home Farm safe

programme.title: See No Evil: The Moors Murders
programme.desc: Conclusion of the dramatisation of the events surrounding the Moors Murders. Ian Brady and Myra Hindley are arrested after damning evidence points to their involvement in the deaths of several missing children from the Manchester area. Myra's sister Maureen Smith struggles to comprehend events, but decides to take to the witness stand to give evidence against the pair

programme.title: See No Evil: The Moors Murders
programme.desc: First in a two-part drama charting the deeds of Moors murderers Ian Brady and Myra Hindley. While searching for missing 12-year-old John Kilbride, investigating officer DCI Mounsey notices a number of children have vanished in the Manchester area. Myra's brother-in-law David is comforted by Brady after the death of his baby daughter - but his misplaced trust leads him to witness a horrifying crime. Maxine Peake and Sean Harris star

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Oscar's golfing pal asks him to investigate the disappearance of his son, who left home after a blazing row with his overbearing father and has been spotted frequenting a club in Whitby. Meanwhile, the Aidensfield police officers are baffled by the activities of a modern-day Robin Hood, who has committed a string of burglaries and given away his ill-gotten gains to the elderly residents of a new block of flats. Drama, starring Derek Fowlds

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.sub-title: The Case of the Missing Will
programme.desc: The sleuth sets out to solve the baffling murder of a Cambridge don who met with foul play shortly after deciding to change his will. Starring David Suchet, Beth Goddard and Mark Kingston

programme.title: The Real Housewives of New York City
programme.desc: Tinsley Mortimer moves back to the city, Ramona fills Dorinda in on the dirt she has about Luann's fiancé Tom, and Sonja throws a Mad Hatter tea party

programme.title: Snowpiercer
programme.desc: Layton learns who was responsible for the experiments on Josie and Liana, before also finding out who froze the world. Big Alice approaches New Eden but can't cross the bridge

programme.title: Winning Combination
programme.desc: Omid Djalili hosts the quiz show which sees nine contestants - Sue, Joe, Elias, Gerry, Michael, Trevor, Alice, Tarryn and John - using their wits to get through to the final - with the combination of numbers they are assigned representing the size of the jackpot they each play to win

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh hosts as Nathan from Bridgend, Michelle from Durham, Tony from Hull and Rachael from Manchester form the brave team willing to take on the challenge, but can they take down one of the country's finest quiz brains?

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Janine, Thomas, Carol and Eli pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Riddiculous
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Ranvir Singh in which Catherine and Emily, Harpreet and Isaac and Scarlett and Megan take on Riddlemaster Henry Lewis. They attempt to answer general knowledge questions and confusing conundrums, with only one team making it through to the final with a chance to win a cash prize

programme.title: Moneyball
programme.desc: Ian Wright hosts as three more teams of two try to bank money by dropping a ball from the Launcher onto an amount of cash. Husband and wife Ben and Helen are aiming for a round-the-world trip, lifelong friends Brian and Andy want to take a nostalgic golfing holiday in style, and mother-and-daughter Shan and Babs hope to take their family to Jamaica to see their relatives

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Kamali, Meg and Katherine compete for the affections of English literature student Harvey, who picks his date according to one whose fashion choices impress him most

programme.title: Storage Hoarders
programme.desc: Aggie MacKenzie sets out to put an end to a self-confessed magpie's compulsive hoarding habit, and begins by talking the avid collector into accepting a valuation for her stash. The presenter also visits west Wales to help a former plumber get rid of his excess clutter as he prepares to begin a new chapter of his life

programme.title: Paddington Station 24/7
programme.desc: Sunday's services grind to a halt when the 11.57 service to Penzance derails as it is departing, while a knife-carrying passenger causes concern in Bristol. Elsewhere, a team of specialists clean the mats laid down to protect the tracks from the actions of passengers ignoring the signs advising them not to flush onboard toilets while the trains are in the station

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.desc: Norway v Sweden (Kick-off 6.00pm).
